revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Burning of the Character Big]][[ja:大文字焼き]]
{{Short description|Japanese Buddhist ritual|qq=}} [[File:HakoneDai0085.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Burning of the Character Big, on Mount Myojo, in the {{ill|Mount Hakone|en|Mount Hakone|qid=Q1067417|lt=Hakone Mountains|1=Mount Hakone}}]] [[File:Gozanokuribi Daimonji3.JPG|thumb|right|200px|The Character Big, on Mount Nyoi, in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}]] The '''Burning of the Character "Big"''' (大), also known as '''Daimonjiyaki''' ({{Langx|ja|大文字焼き|qq=}}) or '''Daimonji Festival''' is the {{ill|Buddhism in Japan|en|Buddhism in Japan|qid=Q736311|lt=Japanese Buddhist|1=Buddhism in Japan}} ritual of burning wood in the character "Big" (大), typically in the mountain, on the last day of the 4-day {{ill|Obon|en|Obon|qid=Q1154888|lt=Obon|1=Obon}} to send back to the other world the spirits of the family ancestors that they welcomed on the first day. There are many locations in Japan where this ritual is held. In western {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, the {{ill|Gozan no Okuribi|en|Gozan no Okuribi|qid=Q2914758|lt=Okuribi of the Five Mountains (''Gozan no Okuribi'')|1=Gozan no Okuribi}} or simply called "Daimonji", celebrated in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}, is the most famous,<ref>[https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/august/daimonji-gozan-okuribi-ritual-fires/ Gozan Okuribi Ritual Fires｜五山送り火 (Discover Kyoto)]</ref> while in eastern Japan, the ritual during the {{ill|Gōra Station|en|Gōra Station|qid=Q997926|lt=Hakone Gora|1=Gōra Station}} Summer Festival with the fire burned on {{ill|Mount Myōjō|en|Mount Myōjō|qid=Q10859199|lt=Mount Myōjō|1=Mount Myōjō}} in the {{ill|Mount Hakone|en|Mount Hakone|qid=Q1067417|lt=Hakone Mountains|1=Mount Hakone}} is relatively well known because of its proximity to {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}.<ref>[https://trip.pref.kanagawa.jp/destination/hakone-gora-summer-festival-daimonji-yaki/1846 Hakone Gora Summer Festival Daimonji-yaki (Kanagawa Prefecture)]</ref> ==See also== *{{ill|Obon|en|Obon|qid=Q1154888|lt=Obon|1=Obon}} *{{ill|Gozan no Okuribi|en|Gozan no Okuribi|qid=Q2914758|lt=Farewell Fires of the Five Mountains|1=Gozan no Okuribi}} in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}} *[[Sagichō Fire Festival]] == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} ==External links== *[https://www.japanese-wiki-corpus.org/culture/Okuribi%20(Ceremonial%20Bonfire%20to%20Send%20out%20Spirits%20of%20the%20Dead).html Okuribi (Ceremonial Bonfire to Send out Spirits of the Dead) (送り火)]
[[en:Burning of the Character Big]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ja:大文字焼き]]
{{wikidata link|Q11435602}} [[Category:Bon Festival]] [[Category:Annual events in Japan]]
